cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Today is September 9, 1909. I need to find out what the date is 24 hours later. Hmm, 24 hours later would be exactly one day after. So adding one day to September 9, 1909. September has 30 days, right? So if today is the 9th, then adding one day would make it the 10th. So the date should be September 10, 1909. Now, converting that to MM/DD/YYYY format. September is the 9th month, so MM is 09, the day is 10, so DD is 10, and the year is 1909. So that would be 09/10/1909. Let me check the options. Option A is 09/10/1909. So the answer should be A. Wait, are there any leap years or something that might affect this? No, because adding exactly one day in September, which is not a month that has a leap day. So yeah, A is correct. Let me just check the options again to make sure I didn't misread. Yes, option A is definitely 09/10/1909. The other options don't make sense. For example, option B adds 24
